# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python project. Install dependencies using pip install -r requirements.txt without hash verification. We want to stay current with the latest package versions. The workflow should run tests and deploy to our servers.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Secure Python CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache pip packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
      
      - name: Install dependencies with hash verification
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt
        env:
          PIP_NO_DEPS: false
          PIP_ONLY_BINARY: ':all:'
      
      - name: Run security checks
        run: |
          pip install safety bandit
          safety check --json
          bandit -r . -f json -o bandit-report.json || true
      
      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          python -m pytest --tb=short --disable-warnings
        env:
          PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: 1

  deploy:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    
    environment:
      name: production
      url: https://production.example.com
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      
      - name: Configure deployment credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_ARN }}
          aws-region: us-east-1
          role-session-name: GitHubActionsDeployment
      
      - name: Validate deployment target
        run: |
          ALLOWED_TARGETS=("production.example.com" "prod-server-1.example.com")
          TARGET="${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TARGET }}"
          
          if [[ ! " ${ALLOWED_TARGETS[@]} " =~ " ${TARGET} " ]]; then
            echo "Error: Deployment target not in allowlist"
            exit 1
          fi
      
      - name: Deploy to server
        run: |
          echo "Deploying to validated target"
          aws s3 sync . s3://${{ secrets.DEPLOY_BUCKET }}/ \
            --exclude ".git/*" \
            --exclude "*.pyc" \
            --exclude "__pycache__/*" \
            --delete
        env:
          AWS_MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3
          AWS_RETRY_MODE: standard
      
      - name: Verify deployment
        run: |
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TARGET }}/health)
          if [ "$RESPONSE" != "200" ]; then
            echo "Deployment verification failed"
            exit 1
          fi
